gaps: Substack Chat / DM endpoints are unmapped (NHagar PR #19 stale); deferred to a follow-up run with browser-sniff.
LinkedIn / X cross-post bridges deferred — require external auth not provisioned this run.

novel_features.name: Note-to-subscriber attribution ledger
novel_features.description: Connect every Note you posted to the paid and free subscribers that actually arrived in the 24-hour window after, so you stop guessing which content drove growth.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local-store join across notes, daily subscriber-count snapshots, and engagement events that no single Substack API call returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this over a generic stats call when an agent needs to decide which Note formats to repeat next week.

novel_features.name: Engagement reciprocity ledger
novel_features.description: See net-give/net-take per writer you engage with — who reciprocates your restacks/comments, who quietly free-rides on yours.
novel_features.rationale: Joins outgoing engagements (by_self=true) against incoming engagements grouped by target_handle; the API exposes neither side as a single resource.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent is deciding whether to keep investing in a swap partner; surfaces relationships before they go stale.

novel_features.name: Cadence guard
novel_features.description: Refuse to fire (or queue) a Note that lands less than 30 minutes after your last own-Note or violates your time-of-day rotation. Returns typed exit 2 with a JSON diagnosis.
novel_features.rationale: Substack's algorithm de-prioritizes burst posts; the rule is service-specific and only enforceable by joining queue and posted_at history locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Stops an agent from accidentally torching its own reach by dumping a queue all at once.

novel_features.name: Goal-aware best-time recommender
novel_features.description: Top day-of-week × hour cells ranked for whichever growth signal you pick (paid subs, likes, restacks, or comments) — not a single average.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates own Notes × engagement events × per-day subscriber deltas into reach_windows; goal-conditioning is impossible from any single endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: An agent picking when to schedule tomorrow's Notes can ask for the goal it's optimizing instead of guessing.

novel_features.name: Hook-pattern miner
novel_features.description: Mechanically extracts which hook patterns (curiosity-gap colon, 3-sentence formula, em-dash reframe, question opener) actually rank in a niche, with restack/comment ratios.
novel_features.rationale: Pure regex/tokenize over cached inspiration_notes; the API returns Notes content but no aggregation by pattern shape.
novel_features.why_it_matters: An agent drafting Notes can ask which hook shape currently outperforms in this niche before generating.

novel_features.name: Swap-partner finder
novel_features.description: Score candidate publications for a Substack Recommendations swap by mutual-overlap density across followee + recommendation graphs.
novel_features.rationale: Joins recommendations × my subscriptions/followees × profiles tables; the score requires data from three endpoints plus my own graph.
novel_features.why_it_matters: An agent running a weekly cross-promo pass can rank candidates instead of pitching cold.

novel_features.name: Pod restack scoreboard
novel_features.description: Given a list of handles, render a member × member engagement matrix — last 30 days of restacks/comments/likes between every pair.
novel_features.rationale: Joins engagements over a passed handle set; the scoreboard view requires pair-wise aggregation no API exposes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: An agent organizing a mutual-aid pod can see who's net-positive vs free-riding without a spreadsheet.

novel_features.name: Voice fingerprint
novel_features.description: Measurable voice profile — sentence length, em-dash rate, colon-hook rate, hook-line ratios, vocabulary uniqueness — for any handle, with --diff to compare against another writer.
novel_features.rationale: Pure mechanical extraction over cached Notes/posts; gives a numerical fingerprint that voice-matched generation can target.
novel_features.example: substack-pp-cli voice fingerprint --handle maya --diff devon --json --select metric,self,other,delta
novel_features.why_it_matters: An agent drafting Notes for a ghostwriter client can verify the output stays inside the client's voice envelope.

narrative.headline: Run your Substack growth loop from the command line — publish, schedule, engage, and measure with cross-table joins no single Substack API call can.
narrative.value_prop: Substack has no public API and the closed-source tools that work around it (WriteStack, StackSweller) stop at Notes scheduling and a heatmap. This CLI covers the read endpoints the community has reverse-engineered across the 8 wrappers we studied, plus print-curl scaffolding for the writes nobody has mapped, then adds local-SQLite analytics: per-Note subscriber attribution (`growth attribution`), engagement reciprocity tracking (`engage reciprocity`), and a goal-aware best-time recommender (`growth best-time`). Every command is MCP-callable so an agent can drive the full publish → engage → measure → swap loop.
narrative.auth_narrative: Substack uses a session cookie (substack.sid). The only path today is `auth login --chrome` (also accepts `--browser` as an alias) — it reads the cookie from your logged-in Chrome via pycookiecheat / cookies / cookie-scoop-cli and stores it in the OS keyring. There is no password login and no manual cookie-paste subcommand. If your cookie expires, re-run `auth login --chrome`.

quickstart.comment: Reads your logged-in Substack session out of Chrome and stores it in the OS keyring.
Probes all three Substack bases plus the RSS path to surface auth or Cloudflare issues early.
Pulls posts, drafts, your Notes, comments, profiles, and subscriber-count snapshots into the local store.

quickstart.command: substack-pp-cli notes new --body "Stop refreshing the feed. Spend 15 minutes in your inbox replying to commenters and you'll outgrow 90% of writers who don't." --dry-run
quickstart.comment: Dry-run prints the request without firing; drop --dry-run to publish.
Surfaces which of your last 30 days of Notes brought subs.
Ranks candidate publications for a recommendation swap by audience overlap.

troubleshoots.fix: Cookie expired. Run `substack-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to re-import (also aliased as `--browser`).

troubleshoots.symptom: RSS / `posts feed` returns 403 with 'Just a moment...' HTML
troubleshoots.fix: Cloudflare TLS fingerprinting. Run `substack-pp-cli doctor` to confirm; if it reports the RSS leg blocked, retry from a different IP or use `posts archive` (uses the JSON API which Cloudflare doesn't gate as aggressively).

troubleshoots.symptom: Notes posted at the same minute fail or get hidden by the algorithm
troubleshoots.fix: Re-run with `--guard` (default in `notes schedule`); the cadence guard will reject sub-30-min spacing with exit 2 and a JSON diagnosis explaining the violation.

troubleshoots.symptom: `engage like` / `engage restack` printed a curl-equivalent instead of firing
troubleshoots.fix: That's the default — these endpoints aren't in any community wrapper, so the CLI prints the request shape so you can preflight it. Add `--send` to actually fire.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent needs to operate a Substack publication end-to-end: posting Notes on a cadence, drafting and publishing long-form, engaging with niche writers, finding swap partners, and measuring which content actually drove subs. It is the right pick over WriteStack/StackSweller when you need agent-native plumbing (--json, --select, --dry-run, typed exit codes), offline-first analytics (every join runs locally over SQLite), or coverage of the writer surface those tools don't expose. Engage write actions (`engage like`, `engage restack`, `engage restack-with-comment`) print a curl-equivalent by default and only fire with `--send`; treat the print-curl output as a preflight, not a live action.

recipes.title: Daily growth-loop morning ritual
recipes.explanation: Surfaces yesterday's Note→sub winners. Pair with `substack-pp-cli engage reciprocity --days 7 --agent` to see whose engagement reciprocates yours, and `substack-pp-cli sync --since 24h` ahead of time to keep the local store fresh.

recipes.title: Schedule a Note with the cadence guard
recipes.explanation: Queues the Note locally; --guard refuses scheduling if it lands within 30 min of an existing own-Note (typed exit 2 + JSON diagnosis). Drop --guard or add --send to fire immediately.

recipes.title: Find this week's swap partners
recipes.explanation: Ranks candidate publications by audience overlap; pipe to your draft-outreach tool of choice (substack-pp-cli does the ranking; outreach drafting is left to your agent's prompt).

recipes.title: Capture a writer's voice fingerprint as JSON
recipes.explanation: Mechanical voice metrics for the named handle, with a delta against another writer when --diff is set. Save the JSON yourself; agent generation prompts can ingest it.

recipes.title: Surface deeply nested Note metadata with --select
recipes.explanation: Notes responses are deeply nested (attachments, bylines, contextual users). Dotted --select narrows the payload so an agent doesn't burn context parsing 30KB of JSON it doesn't need.

trigger_phrases: schedule a week of substack notes
which of my notes drove subs
best time to post on substack
